one_time_description: Treaties and Indigenous Government expenses of $10,520,300 related to legal claims (Surrender, Treaty Benefits, Channelization, Misadministration of Trust) financed by CIBC loans totalling ~$5.8M in new draws; represents a large non-recurring spike vs $2,372,656 prior year
economic_notes: Nation owns 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings LP (dividend $30,000) and 4.41% interest in Kief Cannabis Company Ltd (fully impaired). Operates Muscowpetung Stop & Go Store (convenience store). Pursuing multiple land claim settlements (Surrender, Treaty Benefits, Channelization, Misadministration of Trust) through CIBC loans.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED - auditors unable to obtain sufficient evidence on Muscowpetung Stop & Go Store (Aug 31 year-end used, Sept 1 2021 to Mar 31 2022 period excluded). Massive spike in expenses due to Treaties and Indigenous Government program ($10.5M vs $2.4M prior year) related to legal claim financing. Nation took on ~$18.9M in long-term debt (primarily CIBC loans for land claims), causing net debt to balloon from $10M to $23M and accumulated deficit to worsen from $2.3M to $14M. Current portion of long-term debt is $13.07M creating significant liquidity risk. Financial statements dated July 2023 for March 2022 year-end (15-month delay). Two subsidiaries consolidated: Muscowpetung Saulteaux Business Developments Ltd. and Muscowpetung Ki-Pih-Cheen and Mah-Jahn Convenience Store Inc.
